title: M1 completion gate — full read-only plan lifecycle regression
description: Add a high-level regression test for the complete M1 read-only Planning Muse lifecycle: user planning request creates/stores an awaiting-approval Muse Plan; public plan commands render/list/status/show it; approval and rejection transition only lifecycle/session state; and no shell execution, patch application, workspace writes, or Coding Muse handoff occurs.
acceptance_criteria: Regression covers plan creation, /plan, /plans or /plan history, /plan status, /plan show <id>, /approve plan, /reject plan for a separate awaiting plan/session, no turn execution/handoff/side effects, and focused/full tests as feasible.
notes:
Coordinator: planning-agent-1a6824. Implemented by elixir-expert-39d823.
Implemented by elixir-expert-39d823. Consolidated the existing M1 read-only planning E2E coverage with a completion-gate regression covering slash plan commands plus /approve plan and /reject plan lifecycle. Tests run: mix format lib/muse/session_server.ex test/muse/m1_read_only_planning_test.exs; mix test test/muse/m1_read_only_planning_test.exs; mix test test/muse/session_server_test.exs test/muse/command_dispatcher_test.exs test/muse/conductor_planning_test.exs; mix test.
close_reason: Completed M1 completion-gate regression with deterministic fake Planning Muse plan flow, public plan commands, approval/rejection lifecycle assertions, no execution/handoff/side-effect assertions, and passing focused/full tests.

title: PR 10 — Plan management/history commands
description: Add read-only Muse Plan management/history slash commands for the M1 runtime: /plans, /plan history, /plan status, and /plan show <id>. Preserve /plan active-plan rendering while returning usage errors for unexpected args. Commands must inspect existing context/restored session data or SessionRouter.status/1 only; they must not start turns, execute tools, write workspace files, or mutate plan/session lifecycle state.
acceptance_criteria: /plans and /plan history list all known Muse Plans with active marker, id, status, summary, and task count; /plan status shows active plan lifecycle/session details and timestamps when available; /plan show <id> renders active or historical plans and returns Error: messages for usage/not-found cases; /plan rejects unexpected args without breaking longer /plan subcommands; all new commands are read-only and do not start sessions, turns, tools, shell commands, workspace writes, or lifecycle transitions; command parser/help/autocomplete and dispatcher tests cover the behavior.
notes: Implementation owner: elixir-expert-442cd5. Coordinator: planning-agent-1a6824. Implemented read-only plan history/status/show commands with focused and full test coverage.
close_reason: Implemented read-only Muse Plan management/history commands with parser/help/autocomplete coverage, dispatcher rendering/usage/not-found handling, and router-restored plan history fallback tests.

title: M1 — Read-Only Planning Muse
description:
Milestone/phase epic for M1 — Read-Only Planning Muse. Covers PRs 00, 01a, 01b, 01c, 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07a, 07b, 08, 09, and 10.

Goal: user request creates/resumes a Muse Session; Muse Conductor selects Planning Muse; Prompt.Assembler builds a redacted bundle; fake provider drives the model/tool loop; Planning Muse may use read-only tools only; a structured Muse Plan is persisted and displayed; the session waits for plan approval before any implementation.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/architecture.md; docs/prompts.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: M1 Definition of Done: session created/resumed; Conductor selects Planning Muse; prompt bundle assembled; fake provider drives model/tool loop; read-only list/read/search/git tools are enforced; structured plan is created, persisted, and shown by /plan; session status is :awaiting_plan_approval; CLI/TUI/LiveView can show the plan; zero files modified; zero shell commands; zero implementation handoffs before approval.

title: Muse Universal Runtime v0
description:
Top-level epic for the PLAN.md roadmap to turn Muse into a safe local Muse coding runtime with Muse-first naming, session-aware turns, layered prompting, read-only repository inspection, model/tool-call orchestration, streaming events, approvals, patch application with checkpoints, and CLI/TUI/LiveView visibility.

Child phase epics:
- M1 — Read-Only Planning Muse: PRs 00, 01a, 01b, 01c, 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 07a, 07b, 08, 09, 10.
- Provider/Auth phase: PRs 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16; M4 Real Providers & Auth.
- Patch/Test/Review phase: PRs 17, 18, 19; M2 Basic Coding Muse and M3 Patch Apply, Verification, Rollback.
- Integration/Memory/Extensions phase: PRs 20, 21, 22, 23, 24; M5 and later extension work.

References: PLAN.md; plans/plan-v3-archived.md; docs/architecture.md; docs/prompts.md; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: Muse Runtime v0 Definition of Done from PLAN.md is met: Muse.submit/2 routes through SessionServer + Conductor; sessions persist; prompt previews redact secrets; fake and OpenAI-compatible providers work; Planning Muse uses read-only tools and requires plan approval; Coding Muse proposes patches and requires patch approval; checkpoint/apply/rollback works; safe test runner and Reviewing/Testing Muse flows are bounded; CLI/TUI/LiveView share structured events; no API keys or secrets leak; SessionServer remains responsive and cancellation works.

title: PR10a — Docs/roadmap wording cleanup after PR09
description:
After PR09 (Approval Gate) and PR10 (Plan management/history commands) shipped, several docs still contain stale wording that implies execution-gated behavior is already active:

1. PLAN.md:76 — "Plan approved. Coding Muse will prepare a patch." reads as current behavior; should clarify this describes a post-implementation-gate future experience, not current PR09.
2. docs/architecture.md:1095 — "lib/muse.ex Public API. Delegates submit/resume/approve to SessionServer." overclaims: lib/muse.ex only defines submit/2, not resume or approve.
3. General risk: docs may imply /approve plan starts implementation, Coding Muse handoff, patch apply, shell, network, or writes.

Scope: add plans/pr10a-doc-roadmap-cleanup.md with objective, acceptance checklist, known stale wording, target docs, non-goals, validation plan, and integration checklist. Do not edit code. Avoid broad docs rewrites.

Validation: git diff --check, commit, push.
notes: Coordinator: planning-agent-1a6824. Uses isolated worktree branch pr10a/lane01-tracking-acceptance. Do NOT merge to main.
close_reason: PR10a docs cleanup integrated and pushed to main. All stale wording fixes applied. Coordination completed.

title: PR09 lane11 fake-provider approval gate E2E tests
description: Add deterministic fake-provider E2E/contract coverage for PR09 plan approval/rejection lifecycle without reopening the closed PR09 bead.
close_reason: Added deterministic fake-provider PR09 approval gate E2E coverage and skipped full ApprovalGate hash/session binding contract pending muse-rc8.

title: PR09 full ApprovalGate hardening integration
description: Follow-up integration tracker for PR09 after muse-1ki.1.13 closed with lifecycle-only approval. Deliver full ApprovalGate MVP: implement Muse.Approval and Muse.ApprovalGate, content-bound plan approval/rejection validation with stale prevention, persist approval records/bindings, wire tool gating placeholders safely, and align docs/tests across all PR09 lanes.
close_reason: PR09 approval gate MVP fully integrated and passing all gates: 2667 tests 0 failures, deny-by-default tool policy, content-bound plan approval/rejection, stale prevention, persistence/restore, E2E coverage, safe display/redaction, docs contract. Commit ccaf361 on main.

title: PR09 lane07 CLI approval UX polish
description: Improve slash-command UX around plan approval/rejection without implementing execution. Keep approval output auditable, usage errors safe, docs/tests updated.
close_reason: Implemented CLI approval UX audit output, clear lifecycle usage errors, docs, and regression tests.

title: PR15 post-gate Responses WebSocket fixes
description: Address post-gate audit findings for PR15: gate Responses WebSocket SSE fallback by inbound-frame state, harden WebSocket URL validation, and add regression tests/warning cleanup.
close_reason: Fixed PR15 post-gate WebSocket fallback gating and URL validation; added regression coverage.

title: Fix SSE ReqStream options, redaction, and docs
description: Ensure OpenAI-compatible provider forwards ReqStream request options, ReqStream error summaries redact secrets, and provider roadmap/ReqStream docs match current SSE behavior.
close_reason: Implemented SSE ReqStream option forwarding, transport redaction, and docs fixes; quality gates passed.

title: PR13 Lane 06 — Auth resolver and OpenAI provider integration
description: Implement Muse.Auth.Resolver facade and attach resolved Authorization headers in OpenAICompatibleProvider while preserving explicit headers and keeping tests offline.
close_reason: Auth resolver wired into provider. Commit 2f9da84.

title: Lane 07 PR13 /auth status command hardening
description: Implement the stricter Lane 07 /auth status MVP: shared Commands/CommandDispatcher action, context-aware read-only provider/auth summary, no bearer command/Codex cache side effects, and redaction tests.
close_reason: /auth status delegates to Muse.Auth.Status.render/1. Commit 2f9da84.

title: Plan render lifecycle polish — conditional Muse.Plan.render footer
description: Make Muse.Plan.render/1 footer conditional on plan lifecycle status. Awaiting_approval shows approve/reject guidance; approved shows ready-for-implementation; rejected shows rejection message; terminal statuses show status-specific text; draft/needs_revision avoid misleading approval instructions. Rendering-only, no state mutation.
close_reason: Closed

title: PR 09 — Approval Gate & plan approval
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 09 — Approval Gate & plan approval.

Key scope: implement Approval struct, ApprovalGate, /approve plan, /reject plan, and stale approval prevention.

References: PLAN.md sections 2, 4, 6, 7; docs/architecture.md; docs/security.md; docs/testing.md.
acceptance_criteria: Plan approval/rejection is explicit, stateful, and auditable; stale approvals are rejected; approved plan transition is recorded; rejection is safe; file writes, shell commands, and patch work remain blocked until their later approval gates exist.
notes: Implementation by elixir-expert-9f495f on behalf of planning-agent-1a6824: adding approval-gated /approve plan and /reject plan lifecycle commands.
close_reason: Implemented approval-gated /approve plan and /reject plan lifecycle commands with session/router APIs, dispatcher integration, persistence/events, and tests.

title: PR 08 — Structured plan model & Planning Muse MVP
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 08 — Structured plan model & Planning Muse MVP.

Key scope: add Plan/Task structs, plan parser, validation, /plan command, and session state transition to :awaiting_plan_approval.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/architecture.md; docs/prompts.md; docs/testing.md.
acceptance_criteria: Planning Muse produces a validated structured Muse Plan; plan/tasks are persisted and shown by /plan; invalid plans fail safely; the session enters :awaiting_plan_approval with no implementation handoff.
notes: Lane 04 complete: Planning Muse prompt/model preparation pushed to pr08/lane04-planning-prompt-preparer (commit 4cd2873). 22 new tests. 2449 total tests passing.
close_reason: Implemented conductor/session integration for Planning Muse plan creation. Added plan post-processing to Conductor finalization paths, SessionServer respects result.session.status and stores plans, created 14 new tests covering plan creation, tool-loop plan finalization, invalid plan repair, and PlanParser integration. All 1825 tests pass.

acceptance_criteria: Conductor/TurnRunner work runs outside SessionServer; SessionServer stays responsive; iterative tool-call caps prevent runaway loops; cancellation works; blocked or unsafe tool calls emit clear events and fail safely.

PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 06 — Read-only tools & workspace hardening.

acceptance_criteria: Planning Muse can only access read-only tools before approval; tools cannot write files or run shell commands; workspace boundaries, symlinks, ignored paths, and secret redaction are enforced by Elixir runtime code and covered by tests.

description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 01c — SessionServer & routing.

References: PLAN.md sections 2, 3, 4, 7; docs/architecture.md; docs/testing.md.
acceptance_criteria: Muse.submit/2 remains the public API; sessions create/resume deterministically; SessionServer owns state and event log; Registry/DynamicSupervisor routing works; SessionServer stays responsive and is not coupled to blocking model/tool execution.

title: PR 01b — SessionStore persistence
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 01b — SessionStore persistence.

Key scope: implement crash-safe JSONL persistence, atomic writes, and corrupt-line handling for Muse sessions/events.

References: PLAN.md sections 3, 4, 7; docs/architecture.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: SessionStore persists and replays session/event data safely; writes are atomic; corrupt JSONL lines are handled without crashing the runtime; tests cover happy path, replay, and corruption cases; persisted data avoids secrets.

PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 01a — Event metadata & core structs.

PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 00 — Baseline verification & naming cleanup.

title: Patch/Test/Review phase
description:
Milestone/phase epic for Patch/Test/Review work after Provider/Auth. Covers PRs 17, 18, and 19, and maps to M2 — Basic Coding Muse plus M3 — Patch Apply, Verification, Rollback.

Goal: after explicit plan approval, Coding Muse proposes a patch without writing files; users approve/reject the patch; approved patches create checkpoints, apply safely, support rollback, and can be verified through bounded, approval-aware test/review flows.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/architecture.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md; docs/prompts.md.
acceptance_criteria: M2/M3 Definition of Done: approved plan transitions to Coding Muse; patch proposal includes parseable/formatable diff and stable hash; no file is modified before /approve patch; approved apply creates a Muse Checkpoint, applies the patch, and leaves git diff visible; rollback restores state; safe test commands are bounded and approval-gated; Testing Muse and Reviewing Muse produce findings/recommendations without infinite repair loops.

title: Provider/Auth phase
description:
Milestone/phase epic for Provider/Auth work after the Read-Only Planning Muse milestone. Covers PRs 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, and 16, and maps to M4 — Real Providers & Auth.

Goal: keep fake provider as deterministic test default while adding provider configuration, OpenAI-compatible non-streaming and streaming transports, auth handling, secret redaction, and optional external event delivery for non-LiveView clients.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/security.md; docs/testing.md; docs/architecture.md.
acceptance_criteria: M4-oriented Definition of Done: OpenAI-compatible provider works without replacing fake provider as test default; provider configs and request/response mappers are validated; API keys/bearer auth/Codex cache use are explicit; SSE/WebSocket tool-call and text deltas normalize to the same event model as fake provider; /auth status is safe; provider errors and debug output never leak secrets.

title: PR09 docs/roadmap cleanup — stale wording & API drift
description: Post-PR09 doc cleanup: (1) PLAN.md second product experience now reflects PR09 lifecycle-only approval; (2) architecture.md SessionServer sync calls use exact approve_plan/reject_plan names; (3) architecture.md module map shows actual Muse.submit/2 API surface with slash-command routing; (4) testing.md Coding Muse checklist item scoped as PR17+.
close_reason: All four doc fixes applied, validated, committed, and pushed to pr10a/docs-cleanup.

title: PR09 lane14 UI approval status audit
description: Audit web LiveView, TUI, CLI, EventStream, and dispatcher output paths for PR09 plan approval status/event visibility. Implement low-risk UI-facing fixes or document findings in plans/pr09-ui-approval-status-audit.md. Do not modify the closed PR09 bead lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: Approval status and events are audited across UI/TUI/CLI display paths; UI-facing data does not expose raw plan JSON/secrets; messaging does not imply approval starts implementation; tests or diff checks are run; branch is committed and pushed.
close_reason: Completed UI/TUI/LiveView approval status audit; added display-safe plan lifecycle summaries, tests, and audit doc.

title: Align ApprovalGate docs with implemented PR08 guardrails
description: Lane10 QA found docs/security.md describes a runtime Muse.ApprovalGate enforcing every tool call, but PR08 currently enforces read-only Planning Muse behavior through registry/runner allow-block checks. Either mark ApprovalGate sections as PR09/future architecture or add a minimal ApprovalGate facade before write/shell/network tools land.
acceptance_criteria: docs/security.md accurately distinguishes current PR08 guardrails from PR09 ApprovalGate architecture; no docs claim content-bound approval checks exist until implemented; PR09 issue references the approval binding requirements.
close_reason: Docs now distinguish PR08 registry/runner read-only guardrails from future Muse.ApprovalGate/content-bound approval work; PR08 tests and docs no longer overclaim an implemented ApprovalGate.

title: PR13 auth contract/E2E offline tests
description: Add contract-level offline tests for PR13 auth behavior: OpenAI-compatible requests resolve API key, bearer command, and Codex cache credentials through injected sources, inject only outbound Authorization headers, keep fake provider no-auth/offline, ensure /auth status is safe/read-only, and verify redaction boundaries/no workspace .muse credential writes.
acceptance_criteria: Contract test file test/muse/auth/auth_layer_contract_test.exs covers API key, bearer command, Codex cache, /auth status, fake default, no workspace credential writes, and redaction boundary. Any small auth/provider fixes exposed by these tests are included. Tests/format/compile pass and changes are pushed.
notes: Lane 08 contract tests are pending because Lane 06 (auth resolver/OpenAI provider integration) and Lane 07 (/auth status hardening) remain in_progress and their implementation files are only local uncommitted changes in this workspace, not pulled/pushed on origin/main after a reasonable wait. Do not duplicate their unit tests or commit their in-progress code; resume contract tests after muse-agj and muse-tnp are closed/pushed.
close_reason: Added offline auth layer contract tests covering API key, bearer command, Codex cache, /auth status, fake provider no-auth default, workspace .muse write boundary, and token redaction boundaries. Tests and compile gates pass.

title: PR13 follow-up: wire auth into RequestBuilder Authorization header
description: The auth layer (ApiKey, BearerCommand, CodexCache) resolves credentials but the OpenAI RequestBuilder does not yet inject the Authorization header from the resolved credential. Per PR13 acceptance criteria: 'OpenAI-compatible provider receives Authorization header from auth layer when configured.' Need a new module or wiring in the provider request path that reads the Credential and adds the header to the request spec.
close_reason: Provider auth integration complete. Dependency on PR14 is future work, not a blocker for this PR13 follow-up. Commit 2f9da84.

title: Add PR12 OpenAI-compatible provider contract tests
description: Retry PR12 provider boundary coverage after core implementation: add contract-level tests for OpenAICompatibleProvider.complete/2 using injected offline post_fn, asserting request builder/mapper/decoder integration, provider-ready JSON, custom base_url endpoint composition, decoded Response normalization, no network calls, schema debug-name stripping, and redaction boundaries without duplicating unit tests.
close_reason: Added OpenAI-compatible provider contract tests for complete/2 offline post_fn boundary, provider-ready JSON, custom base_url composition, decoder normalization including tool calls, and redaction boundary. Gates passed: mix format; focused contract test; OpenAI provider/router suite; mix compile --warnings-as-errors.

title: Lane 06 provider router for LLM provider selection
description: Add a pure, offline-testable provider router for explicit LLM provider-module selection. Keep the safe fake default unchanged and avoid depending on network/auth/provider implementation work from later PRs.
acceptance_criteria: Provider router maps fake and openai_compatible provider identifiers/configs to provider modules without creating atoms from strings; unknown providers return explicit errors; safe fake default remains offline-testable; focused tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented ProviderRouter with offline tests and conservative Conductor router integration; quality gates passed.

title: RequestBuilder should strip/validate URL credentials from base_url
description: During PR12 investigation, RequestBuilder.build_url/1 was found to accept and return URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.test/v1) without stripping or rejecting them. These credentials would persist in the returned request spec and could leak if the spec ends up in logs, errors, or events. This should be fixed when the OpenAICompatibleProvider is created: either reject base_url values with userinfo, or strip credentials before constructing the URL.
close_reason: Closed

title: Read-only Planning Muse — zero writes/shell E2E
description:
## Summary

End-to-end integration test ensuring Planning Muse can inspect/list/read/search and produce a structured plan with zero workspace writes and zero shell execution.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Session ends in :awaiting_plan_approval with a %Muse.Plan{} in status
- [ ] Plan status is :awaiting_approval
- [ ] Plan is persisted through Muse.SessionStore
- [ ] Flow includes read-only inspection/tool activity (list/read/search/git read-only)
- [ ] Workspace contents are unchanged before/after: no new files, no modified files
- [ ] No shell/tool events for write, patch apply, command execution, or workspace mutation occur
- [ ] No execution handoff to Coding Muse occurs
- [ ] Running /plan, /plans, /plan status, and /plan show <id> after plan creation are read-only

Coordinator: planning-agent-1a6824
notes: elixir-expert-clone-2-745389 finishing for coordinator planning-agent-1a6824: added git_status/git_diff_readonly tool coverage to M1 read-only planning E2E, git repo init in seed_workspace, .git/ exclusion in workspace_snapshot, patch_propose in forbidden lists. All 1892 tests green.
close_reason: Added git_status/git_diff_readonly read-only git tool coverage to M1 planning E2E. Git repo initialized in seed_workspace, .git/ excluded from snapshot. patch_propose added to forbidden lists. All 1892 tests green. Commit b6c78ee.

title: Document Muse Plan lifecycle commands
description: Update user-facing CLI command docs so Muse Plan lifecycle commands added in PR08-10 are visible in README/help-oriented documentation. Keep changes isolated to docs/README and use Muse-first wording.
acceptance_criteria: README/docs list /plan, /plans, /plan history, /plan status, /plan show <id>, /approve plan, and /reject plan with Muse-first wording; no runtime code changes.
close_reason: Documented Muse Plan lifecycle commands in README and canonical architecture command snippets.

PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 18 — Patch apply, checkpoint, rollback.

PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 17 — Coding Muse patch proposal.

PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 16 — Optional external WebSocket channel.

title: PR 15 — Responses WebSocket provider
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 15 — Responses WebSocket provider.

Key scope: implement persistent Responses WebSocket support, previous_response_id handling, and SSE fallback.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/architecture.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: Responses WebSocket can connect when configured, preserve/use previous_response_id as designed, fall back to SSE when needed, and normalize all provider events without leaking credentials or provider debug secrets.
close_reason: Closed

title: PR 14 — HTTP SSE provider
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 14 — HTTP SSE provider.

Key scope: implement SSE parser, event normalizer, and streaming text/tool-call delta support for HTTP streaming providers.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/architecture.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: SSE streams parse incrementally; provider text deltas and tool-call deltas normalize to the same event types as fake provider; stream errors are redacted and recoverable; CLI/TUI/LiveView consumers can render the normalized events.
notes: PR14 integrated and complete. SSE streaming path implemented in OpenAICompatibleProvider.stream/2. 2336 tests, 0 failures. See commit 1433bec for details.
close_reason: Closed

title: PR 13 — Auth layer
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 13 — Auth layer.

Key scope: support API key auth, bearer command auth, Codex cache bridge, and /auth status while protecting tokens and OAuth material.

References: PLAN.md sections 2, 4, 6, 7; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria:
Auth credential model includes safe redacted inspect/status output (no raw secrets).
API key resolution order covers explicit env map, system env, provider config, and app config; initial key name MUSE_OPENAI_API_KEY; values never leaked.
Bearer command auth supports non-interactive timeout, exit-status handling, bounded output parsing, and redaction; no hanging prompts.
Codex cache bridge reads ~/.codex/auth.json or explicit path with permission warning/check; tests use fake fixtures only; token values never logged.
/auth status reports configured provider auth state/source/redacted status with no raw secrets.
OpenAI-compatible provider receives Authorization header from auth layer when configured; fake provider remains default and tests stay offline.
No token persisted under workspace .muse/; no real network tests; all auth errors/events are bounded and redacted.
notes: Coordination: planning-agent-1a6824 is running the 12-lane PR13 implementation. Lane 01/12 metadata sync by light-puppy-b13366; no runtime code changes performed.
close_reason: PR13 — Auth layer: Credential struct, ApiKey resolver, BearerCommand resolver, CodexCache bridge, /auth status command, all with redacted inspect/safety. 2104 tests pass. See commits b21ab1a, a3bf25b, 8750003.

title: PR 12 — OpenAI-compatible non-streaming provider
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 12 — OpenAI-compatible non-streaming provider.

Key scope: implement encoder/decoder, req-based HTTP integration, custom base_url support, and redacted provider errors for OpenAI-compatible non-streaming calls.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/security.md; docs/testing.md.
acceptance_criteria: Configured OpenAI-compatible non-streaming provider can make requests against a custom base_url; decoding normalizes to provider structs/events; network use is opt-in/configured; failures are explicit and redacted; fake provider remains default for offline tests.
notes:
[2026-05-10] Investigation complete. Muse.LLM.OpenAICompatibleProvider is absent from main — PR11 (muse-1ki.2.1) has not been merged, no branch exists locally or remotely. RequestBuilder exists but is pure data prep only. Security redaction tests for provider errors/decoding require the provider module to exist. Blocked on PR11 delivery. Potential concern: RequestBuilder.build_url/1 passes URLs with embedded credentials (user:pass@host) through without stripping — should be addressed when provider is created.
[code-puppy-66714f] Lane 04 continuing: provider adapter files absent from main despite bead closure; implementing OpenAI-compatible non-streaming provider core.
close_reason: PR12 implementation complete: OpenAICompatibleProvider + ProviderRouter + ChatCompletionsDecoder + RequestBuilder + Req integration committed and pushed to main. All 2047 tests pass (0 failures). Pushed as 9c7045d.

title: PR 11 — Provider config & request mappers
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 11 — Provider config & request mappers.

Key scope: implement ProviderConfig, Responses/ChatCompletions JSON mappers, and secret redaction for provider configuration and wire payloads.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/security.md; docs/testing.md.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configs validate required fields and fail safely; Responses and Chat Completions request/response mapping is covered by tests; prompt previews, logs, events, and errors redact API keys, bearer tokens, and other secrets.
notes: Lane 02/12 (elixir-expert-0c967c): ProviderConfig hardened with pure result-returning env-map load/from_env API, structured invalid env errors, required model/base_url validation for openai_compatible, no API key storage, and secret-safe redacted/raw inspect coverage. Focused ProviderConfig and relevant LLM/prompt tests passing.
close_reason: Muse.Config module implemented: resolves LLM provider config from env map/app config, defaults to fake, delegates validation to ProviderConfig.validate/1, no network/auth side effects, clear error messages for invalid provider/URL/model/timeout. 29 async tests passing. Flaky Process.list test removed.

title: Integration/Memory/Extensions phase
description:
Milestone/phase epic for Integration/Memory/Extensions work after Patch/Test/Review. Covers PRs 20, 21, 22, 23, and 24, and maps to M5 plus later extension/backlog work.

Goal: polish CLI/TUI/LiveView integration, add Memory/Restoration Muse and handoff foundations, complete developer docs, broaden provider/model routing, and prepare a future remote-execution abstraction while preserving the v0 safety contract.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10; docs/architecture.md; docs/prompts.md; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md; README.md.
acceptance_criteria: M5/later Definition of Done: CLI/TUI/LiveView share unified commands and Muse-first strings; session panels/events are coherent; memory compaction, Restoration Muse, and controlled handoffs exist only after the local runtime is safe; docs cover setup, safety, provider use, and architecture; additional provider routing preserves redaction and approval rules; remote execution remains local-only/denied-by-default until a later approved milestone with strict approvals.

title: PR16: Optional WS client dep
description: Add optional WS client dependency for production WebSocket.Stream.default_stream/3

title: PR13 follow-up: add bearer command timeout option
description: BearerCommand.resolve/1 lacks a timeout option. Per PR13 acceptance criteria: 'Bearer command auth supports non-interactive timeout.' Add a :timeout_ms opt (default e.g. 10000) that uses Process.spawn + receive/after to enforce a deadline on System.cmd, preventing hanging prompts from blocking the auth pipeline.
close_reason: BearerCommand now has timeout_ms option (default 5000ms) with spawn+monitor isolation. :enoent and other process DOWN reasons mapped to safe error messages. Commit 2f9da84.

title: PR 23 — Additional providers & model routing
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 23 — Additional providers & model routing.

Key scope: add OpenRouter, Ollama, Anthropic, and per-Muse model pinning/model routing after the core provider path is safe.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 6, 10; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/security.md; docs/testing.md; docs/prompts.md.
acceptance_criteria: Additional provider presets integrate with the same Provider behavior and event normalization; per-Muse model pinning is configurable and testable; fake provider remains available; redaction, approvals, and safety constraints remain provider-independent.

title: PR 22 — Documentation & developer onboarding
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 22 — Documentation & developer onboarding.

Key scope: update README and developer docs for provider setup, safety model, architecture, testing, and roadmap expectations.

References: PLAN.md sections 8, 10; README.md; docs/architecture.md; docs/provider-roadmap.md; docs/testing.md; docs/security.md; docs/prompts.md.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains setup, fake-provider-first testing, provider/auth configuration, safety/approval model, architecture/process supervision, prompt profiles, and developer onboarding; docs reflect Muse-first terminology and current behavior.

title: PR 21 — Memory & Restoration Muse, handoffs
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 21 — Memory & Restoration Muse, handoffs.

Key scope: add memory compaction, memory artifact support as specified by PLAN.md, Restoration Muse behavior, and specialist handoffs via Muse Conductor.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 5, 6, 7, 10; docs/prompts.md; docs/architecture.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: Memory Muse compacts session context safely; Restoration Muse can diagnose failures and restore checkpoints; Conductor handoffs are explicit and controlled; memory/restoration work happens only after the local runtime safety contract is stable; secrets are not persisted in memory artifacts.

title: PR 20 — CLI/TUI/LiveView integration polish
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 20 — CLI/TUI/LiveView integration polish.

Key scope: unify commands across CLI/TUI/LiveView, add session panels, and ensure Muse-first strings everywhere.

References: PLAN.md sections 4, 5, 7; docs/architecture.md; README.md; docs/security.md.
acceptance_criteria: CLI, TUI, and LiveView share command semantics and event rendering; session panels show relevant state; user-facing text uses Muse-first terminology everywhere; no Agent/Bot/mascot labels are introduced.

title: PR 24 — Remote execution (later)
description:
PR roadmap item from PLAN.md: PR 24 — Remote execution (later).

Key scope: introduce a runner abstraction and local runner foundation for possible future SSH/remote execution, while keeping remote execution denied until a later approved milestone.

References: PLAN.md sections 2, 4, 9, 10; docs/architecture.md; docs/security.md; docs/testing.md.
acceptance_criteria: Runner abstraction supports local execution boundaries first; any future SSH/remote execution remains out of v0/local safety scope unless separately approved; remote execution is denied by default; strict approvals and observability are part of the design before enabling non-local runners.
